content.problemStatement:
You are given a **permutation** P=[P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN]P = [P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N]P=[P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč] of {1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N\}{1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N}.

Count the number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that:


1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq j \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N
i+ji+ji+j divides Pi+PjP_i+P_jPi‚Äč+Pj‚Äč.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated distinct integers P1,P2,P3,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, P_3, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,P3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs satisfying the given condition.
constraints: 1‚Č§Pi‚Č§N1 \leq P_i \leq N1‚Č§Pi‚Äč‚Č§N for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Valid pairs are (1,1),(1,2)(1,1),(1,2)(1,1),(1,2) and (2,2)(2,2)(2,2).
Test case 222: (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) is the only valid pair.
Test case 333: Valid pairs are (1,1),(2,2)(1,1),(2,2)(1,1),(2,2) and (3,4)(3,4)(3,4).
